title: Oral Healthcare Chain, Eka Dental Care plans 25+ Clinics by FY27
summary: India’s healthcare market, especially oral care, is quite fragmented – from economic local clinics, to premium oral care wings at multispecialty hospitals. Brands like Eka Dental Care offer the consistency […]
thbAction: Map the news to THB's provider CRM for referral growth, patient lifecycle engagement, and multi-facility relationship management across hospital and provider networks.
contentHtml:
Currently operating self-owned clinics across Mumbai, Thane, Navi Mumbai, and Dombivli, the company will deepen its presence in the Mumbai Metropolitan Region (MMR) while entering Tier 1 cities such as Pune and Bengaluru.

India’s healthcare market, especially oral care, is quite fragmented – from economic local clinics, to premium oral care wings at multispecialty hospitals. Brands like Eka Dental Care offer the consistency of a premium dental practice at affordable rates, which is critical to expanding healthcare access in a country like India which is plagued by the lack of quality healthcare.

Eka Dental’s expansion is structured around two facets – delivering a consistent oral care practice; and leveraging technology solutions. The company, for example, is sourcing best-in-class machinery and equipment; and is also developing a proprietary AI platform to create treatment plans and patient management. Eka Dental clinics charge 40 to 60 per cent less than a comparable private practice in Mumbai; and hence, fulfil a significant gap between the premium dental chains and the unorganized, local clinics with an organized, economic and premium dental care. 

According to the Company, it observed a 25-30 per cent patient traffic surge in Quarter 1, 2026, driven by new clinics and strategic marketing efforts. Through this expansion drive, Eka Dental Care plans to scale patient volume by 2 to 2.5X in the next 12-18 months. Currently, Eka Dental Care operates on a company-owned model to ensure patient experience consistency as well as comply with service quality standards. However, the company is open to structured franchise or partnership opportunities as the brand matures.

Commenting on the expansion, Sachin Katira, Co-Founder & CEO, Eka Dental Care, said: “Utilizing AI and technology for treatment planning and patient management, we are not only improving our patients’ clinical results but we are also creating a more efficient and reliable patient experience, which is by far highly organized in a nascent but highly optimistic healthcare market like India.”

Dr. Jeevan Shetty, Co-Founder & COO, Eka Dental Care, added: “A huge need exists in the market for reliable and affordable dental services that will provide patients with confidence and trust in their providers. At Eka Dental Care, we’re combining clinical quality with the use of standardized protocols, high-quality technology, and an emphasis on patient hygiene and ethical behavior to provide this solution.”

Eka Dental Care’s cluster-led expansion creates operational and brand consistency at scale throughout our network of clinics and creates an environment that ensures that we deliver an equal level of clinical care to our patients regardless of which clinic they visit.
